title: Operator decision: symlink direction for paste-cache vault (~/.claude/paste-cache/ <-> mono vault)
description:
vaults/paste-cache/ was seeded at 3e23ff564f as a non-destructive copy of ~/.claude/paste-cache/. 29 files (1.1MB) now mirrored at private/universal/vaults/paste-cache/files/. Original location at ~/.claude/paste-cache/ was preserved untouched.

Operator decision needed before any destructive move on the original location:

Option A — substrate canonical. Move files from ~/.claude/paste-cache/ to mono vault, symlink ~/.claude/paste-cache/ -> mono vault. Claude Code's future paste-cache writes land directly in mono vault for auto-tracking + auto-commit. Pro: substrate is canonical home. Con: depends on the mono-vault absolute path being stable.

Option B — original canonical. Mono vault is a periodically-refreshed snapshot. ~/.claude/paste-cache/ stays canonical; substrate vault is mirror. Pro: matches Claude Code's existing assumption. Con: substrate vault becomes stale; needs sync mechanism.

Option C — dual + sync via Swift CLI. Both stay separate; Swift CLI periodically syncs mono vault from original (or vice versa). Pro: zero coupling. Con: two sources of truth; divergence risk.

Closure: operator picks A/B/C; execute the picked option; update vaults/paste-cache/index.md to reflect the decision; if A, do the destructive move + symlink atomically with backup beforehand; if C, author the Swift sync CLI.
